title: Iveco Daily Luton Van
body:
[![Iveco Daily Luton Van](../images/Iveco_Daily_35S14H_LWB_140ps_Business_Luton_Box_Tail_Lift_front1-e1744885927750-600x360.webp)](https://www.vansales.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Iveco_Daily_35S14H_LWB_140ps_Business_Luton_Box_Tail_Lift_front1-e1744885927750.webp)

Iveco Daily Luton Van Review The Iveco Daily Luton Van ranks among the biggest commercial vans you can buy today. We’ve spent six months getting a full picture of this…

biggest commercial vans you can buy today. We’ve spent six months getting a full picture of this vehicle, and now we’re ready to share our detailed Iveco Daily review. This powerhouse gives you great value at £450 + VAT, with an impressive engine lineup ranging from to horsepower.

Our team put this van to the test in commercial settings of all sizes. The separate chassis and body construction grabbed our attention, especially when we loaded it with heavy cargo. On top of that, the model comes with a refined interior. A leather-trimmed steering wheel and smooth device connectivity for both Android and Apple make our everyday drives more comfortable.

From its eco-friendly CNG option to its smart storage solutions, we’ve tested every aspect of this van over the past six months. Our findings will show you if this Luton van lives up to its claims. Business owners who want to know if this investment makes sense will find answers in our detailed review.

## Months with the Iveco Daily Luton: What We’ve Learned

My six months with the Iveco Daily Luton have given me deep insights into this capable vehicle. Long-term testing reveals subtle details that quick reviews often miss.

### Daily driving experience across city and motorway

The Daily Luton gives you a commanding view of the road, which helps a lot when you’re manoeuvring through tight city streets. You can spot hazards early and read traffic patterns better than in smaller vans. The power steering makes this big vehicle surprisingly easy to handle.

This van stays rock-solid on motorways, even in strong crosswinds – a big plus when you’re driving something this tall. Our test model’s.3-litre engine gets up to speed fine for merging, though you might need to downshift on steep hills with a full load. The cruise control is a lifesaver on long trips and helps keep fatigue at bay.

The six-speed manual transmission deserves praise. Each gear shift clicks into place with precision, and the ratios match the engine’s power delivery perfectly. City driving needs more attention to gear selection at first, but it becomes automatic after a few weeks.

The cabin stays quiet most of the time, though the large wing mirrors create some wind noise. The suspension hits a sweet spot – it’s firm enough to keep the van stable yet soft enough to smooth out most bumps.

### Payload handling during removals and deliveries

The Daily Luton really shows its strength when it comes to carrying loads. Its separate chassis design keeps heavy cargo stable – a vital feature when you’re moving delicate items. The van handles beautifully even at maximum payload, which I’ve tested through countless removal jobs.

The cargo space works great for moving furniture. There’s plenty of height for tall items like wardrobes and fridges. The rear doors open wide for easy loading, though the side door could be a bit wider for those awkward pieces.

I found an unexpected bonus in the wheel arch design. They barely cut into the cargo space, which makes loading wide furniture or pallets much easier. The cargo area has tie-down points right where you need them, so securing loads is straightforward.

The optional rear air suspension costs more upfront but has paid for itself many times over, especially with uneven loads. Yes, it is great to adjust the ride height when you’re dealing with loading bays of different heights.

## Interior Comfort and Driver Usability Over Time

The Iveco Daily Luton’s true character emerges only after spending extended time behind the wheel. Our six-month test period helped me learn about how the cabin features hold up during commercial use.

### Cabin ergonomics during long shifts

Life in the Daily Luton’s cabin shows that comfort during extended shifts comes with trade-offs. The memory foam seats look simple but have kept their support well through hundreds of driving hours. The padding could be more generous for longer trips. My investment in the optional suspended driver’s seat paid off as it smooths out harsh road bumps that would tyre you out.

The.25-inch digital instrument cluster displays vital information clearly. Some secondary data can be harder to read quickly. I love that I can switch the speedometer between MPH and KM/H for international trips – a practical feature that works great for cross-border operations.

Adjustability stands out as a major strength. The steering wheel adjustments and three-way adjustable driver’s seat let me stay comfortable even after hours of driving. The leather-rimmed steering wheel’s flat bottom design gives me more elbow room during turns.

### Storage and layout for tools and documents

The Daily Luton’s practical storage solutions proved valuable during our testing. The cabin comes with storage compartments, ranging from door bins to specialised spaces like the smartphone and tablet holder with USB and USB-C charging ports.

The centre passenger seat folds down to create a desk – perfect to complete paperwork or eat lunch between deliveries. A cup holder near the A-pillar keeps drinks close, and the dashboard’s charging ports are essential to keep devices powered.

The modest glovebox with its extra shelf space handles all the paperwork from daily operations well. The under-seat tool tray took some time to figure out, but once organised properly, it keeps tools secure without rattling.

## Fuel Economy, Maintenance and Running Costs

Our six-month test of the Iveco Daily Luton needed careful tracking of wallet costs. I kept detailed records of everything from fuel stops to workshop visits to give future buyers a clear picture.

### Ground fuel consumption after,000 miles

The Iveco Daily Luton shows mixed results for fuel efficiency. Our.3L engine reached the,000-mile mark with a combined average of **25 MPG**. These numbers line up with what you’d expect from this type of vehicle, though some rivals do better.

City driving gave us **24.4 MPG** when loaded. The numbers improved on highways to **28.7 MPG**. The Daily’s reliable, truck-style construction explains these figures. Iveco claims their new model uses% less fuel than older versions, but full loads cut these numbers down quite a bit.

The updates brought a% boost in efficiency. New features like Michelin eco tyres, electronic power steering, and variable-geometry turbos made this possible. The Eco button helps save fuel by limiting top speed, which works great on steady highway runs.

### Service intervals and maintenance experience

The Daily’s truck heritage makes maintenance easier than expected. A standard five-year,,000-mile warranty gives peace of mind. Service intervals stretch up to,000 miles between visits, which cuts down on garage time substantially.

The maintenance schedule has:

-   Engine oil and philtre changes yearly or every,000 km
-   M1 service at,000 km
-   Extended M2 service at,000 km

IVECO-approved oils stretch service intervals to,000 km instead of,000 km with regular oils. This saves money over time. The dealer network stays open on Sundays, which makes scheduling maintenance around busy workdays much easier.

The Daily might drink more fuel, but shorter downtimes and flexible dealer hours make the total running costs better than they first appear.

## Who This Van Suits Best After Months of Use

My six months driving the Iveco Daily Luton has revealed its true character and shown exactly which businesses get the most value from this strong workhorse.

### Ideal for couriers, tradespeople and removals

The largest longitudinal study shows the Iveco Daily Luton works best for businesses that handle big loads day in and day out. Courier companies love its massive interior space. The load volume reaches up to.3m³, which beats the Citroen Relay Luton’s.8m³ capacity easily. The van’s unique over-cab “peak” design adds extra storage space that’s perfect to secure fragile items or organise delivery paperwork.

This van has become a removal company’s best friend. Its size easily fits bulky furniture, and the optional tail lift makes loading heavy items much simpler – something I’ve really come to value after countless house moves. The solid chassis handles weight distribution beautifully, which matters a lot when you’re moving household goods around.

Tradespeople who haul heavy materials will find the Daily Luton’s payload capacity impressive. It ranges fromkg to,210kg based on how it’s configured. This hefty capacity combined with the van’s toughness makes it perfect to carry tools, building materials, or trade equipment over long distances.

### Where it may fall short for other users

Solo traders who work only in city areas might struggle with the Daily Luton’s size. While great for capacity, it can be tough to manoeuvre in tight city centres or limited parking spots. Businesses that don’t need to carry heavy loads might end up paying for capacity they’ll never use.

The Daily Luton drinks more fuel than smaller alternatives – that’s something to consider if fuel economy tops your priority list. Without regular heavy loads, many of the van’s structural strengths become extras you’re paying for but don’t need.

Companies that need quick side access should know something important. While the rear doors are great for loading, the side door might be too small for wider items. This can slow things down if you need to load from the side often.

## Conclusion

The Iveco Daily Luton proves to be a solid choice for specific business needs after six months of thorough testing. The van’s strong construction and impressive payload capacity make up for its higher fuel consumption of MPG compared to competitors.

Long workdays become easier with the van’s separate chassis construction and well-thought-out interior features. The folding centre desk and multiple storage options add convenience. The service intervals stretch up to,000 miles, which means fewer workshop visits and less downtime to offset your investment.

This van works best for removal companies and courier services that handle big loads often. Smaller businesses or city-based operations might want to look at more compact options. The Daily Luton’s best feature is how well it handles both motorway runs and heavy payloads with consistent performance.

The Iveco Daily Luton gives great value to businesses that need a reliable workhorse, despite its city driving limits and fuel costs. Its practical features combine with excellent load capacity and build quality to create a smart investment for the right business.

## FAQs

**Q1. What is the average fuel consumption of the Iveco Daily Luton?** Based on extensive testing, the Iveco Daily Luton averages around MPG combined. Urban driving yields approximately.4 MPG when loaded, while motorway journeys improve slightly to.7 MPG.

**Q2. How often does the Iveco Daily Luton require servicing?** The Iveco Daily Luton has extended service intervals of up to,000 miles between workshops. Engine oil and philtre changes are recommended every,000 km or annually, with an M1 service every,000 km and an extended M2 service every,000 km.

**Q3. What is the payload capacity of the Iveco Daily Luton?** The Iveco Daily Luton offers an impressive payload capacity, typically ranging betweenkg and,210kg depending on the specific configuration. This substantial capacity makes it ideal for businesses transporting heavy materials or equipment.

**Q4. How does the Iveco Daily Luton perform in urban environments?** While the Iveco Daily Luton offers excellent visibility and responsive handling, its large size can be challenging in tight urban spaces. It’s more suited to businesses that require ample cargo space and frequently transport heavy loads, rather than those operating exclusively in city centres.

**Q5. What types of businesses benefit most from using the Iveco Daily Luton?** The Iveco Daily Luton is particularly well-suited for courier companies, removal businesses, and tradespeople who regularly handle substantial loads. Its spacious interior, robust construction, and impressive payload capacity make it ideal for transporting bulky items, furniture, and heavy materials over long distances.
